## Summary

Describe the user-visible or engineering outcome and why it belongs in Flowtype.

## Change Type

- [ ] Swift app or UI
- [ ] Qwen helper or model integration
- [ ] Post-processing or transcription behaviour
- [ ] Tests, build, packaging, or CI
- [ ] Documentation only

## Verification

List the narrow and broad commands run, plus results. Do not claim GUI, installed-app, signing, notarization, or DMG verification unless it was actually performed.

## Privacy And Release Boundaries

- [ ] No real recordings, transcripts, credentials, signing material, private paths, model files, caches, or generated environments are included.
- [ ] New network calls, telemetry, permissions, persistence, or retention changes are documented and tested, or this change adds none.
- [ ] Third-party code and assets have compatible provenance and notices, or this change adds none.
- [ ] User-facing behaviour changes include relevant tests or a concrete manual test script.

## Notes For Maintainers

The public repository is a deterministic projection of a private source of truth. Maintainers may reproduce an accepted change upstream and publish an equivalent exported commit instead of merging this commit directly.
